items.title: Porro signs new long-term Tottenham contract
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur right-back Pedro Porro signs a new, long-term contract with the club understood to be valid until 2031.

items.summary: OMAR ARTAN will still receive his full pay despite being blocked from entering the USA to referee at the World Cup. items.title: England defeat Scotland to book spot in World Cup of Darts final!
items.summary: Luke Humphries and Luke Littler see off Cameron Menzies and Gary Anderson to book a spot in the World Cup of Darts final.

items.title: Robinson ruled out of England's second Test against New Zealand
items.summary: Ollie Robinson has been ruled out of England's second Test against New Zealand due to soreness in his right knee.

items.title: Giroud: Pulisic reminded me of Hazard at Chelsea
items.summary: Olivier Giroud has said his former Chelsea and AC Milan teammate Christian Pulisic "reminded him of Eden Hazard."

items.summary: Germany made a confident start to their World Cup campaign with a 7-1 win over tournament debutants Curacao in Group E.

items.summary: India got their ICC Women's T20 World Cup campaign off to the perfect start with a 64-run victory over Pakistan at Edgbaston.

items.summary: DICK ADVOCAAT couldn't hold back the tears as Curacao embarked on their first ever World Cup match. items.title: FIFA: 'Technical outage' led to VAR controversy
items.summary: FIFA have confirmed that a "technical outage" meant that VAR's semi-automated offside technology was not able to be used for the controversial call that led to Switzerland's opening goal against Qatar on Saturday.

items.title: Sources: Real Madrid agree $69M Cucurella deal
items.summary: Real Madrid have agreed a deal worth up to £51.7 million (€60 million/$69.3 million) with Chelsea to sign Marc Cucurella, sources have told ESPN.

items.summary: Enjoy all the action from Sunday's World Cup matches, with features, news, video analysis and color from around the competition.

items.title: Sources: Somali ref to receive full World Cup pay
items.summary: Somali referee Omar Artan will be paid for his World Cup match assignments by FIFA, sources have told ESPN, despite being refused entry into the United States prior to the start of the tournament.

items.title: Havertz double leads Germany in WC opening rout
items.summary: Kai Havertz scored two goals as Germany pulled away from World Cup first-timer Curaçao late in the opening half and piled on after that in a 7-1 win Sunday in a group match.

items.title: Copy of Australia shock Türkiye to go level with USMNT
items.summary: Nestory Irankunda and Connor Metcalfe scored and Australia spoiled Türkiye's return to the World Cup for the first time in 24 years with a 2-0 victory on Saturday night.
